pi_items.source: Adobe Commerce official documentation (https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/commerce-admin/systems/security/security-admin) specifies Password Lifetime in days. 90 days is a standard enterprise security practice for password rotation policies.
Adobe Commerce documentation confirms 'Forced' option requires admin users to change passwords after account setup. Value '1' represents the 'Forced' setting (boolean equivalent).

Adobe Commerce documentation specifies 'Maximum Login Failures to Lockout Account' with default of 6 attempts. Value 5 is a valid configuration stricter than default, appropriate for security hardening.

Adobe Commerce documentation specifies 'Lockout Time (minutes)' determines duration an admin account remains locked after max login failures. 30 minutes is a standard and reasonable security timeout value.

Adobe Commerce documentation specifies 'Admin Session Lifetime (seconds)' with minimum of 60 seconds. 1800 seconds equals 30 minutes. Default is 900 seconds (15 minutes), but 1800 is within valid range and represents extended session security.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Magento Admin Security at Stores > Configuration > Advanced > Admin > Security with five verified parameters: Password Lifetime set to 90 days (standard enterprise password rotation policy), Password Change set to Forced (value 1, requiring mandatory password updates), Maximum Login Failures to Lockout Account set to 5 (stricter than 6-attempt default), Lockout Time set to 30 minutes (standard account lock duration), and Admin Session Lifetime set to 1800 seconds or 30 minutes (exceeds default 15-minute timeout for enhanced security). All values are valid Magento configuration parameters within documented ranges.
